records.ety: [L. officialis: cf. F. officiel. See Office, and cf. Official, n.]

defs: Of or pertaining to an office or public trust; as, official duties, or routine.
quotes: That, in the official marks invested, you Anon do meet the senate.

defs: Derived from the proper office or officer, or from the proper authority; made or communicated by virtue of authority; as, an official statement or report.
Approved by authority; sanctioned by the pharmacopoeia; appointed to be used in medicine; as, an official drug or preparation. Cf. Officinal.

quotes: The stomach and other parts official unto nutrition.

records.ety: [L. officialis a magistrate's servant or attendant: cf. F. official. See Official, a., and cf. Officer.]

defs: One who holds an office; esp., a subordinate executive officer or attendant.
An ecclesiastical judge appointed by a bishop, chapter, archdeacon, etc., with charge of the spiritual jurisdiction.
